The code is consistent with standard patterns.\n\n---\n\n### Scores and Justifications:\n\n#### **Malware Score: 0 in all reports**  \n**Justification:**  \n- No signs of malicious code, such as network calls to suspicious domains, data exfiltration, or backdoors.  \n- The bytecode is a standard compiled contract, not obfuscated or suspicious.  \n- The code only performs contract deployment and connection, typical for such factory classes.\n\n#### **Obfuscated Score: 0 in all reports**  \n**Justification:**  \n- The code is straightforward, auto-generated, with no obfuscation or complex logic.\n\n#### **Security Risk Score: Ranges from 0.1 to 0.2**  \n**Justification:**  \n- The code itself is low risk; it’s a utility for deploying and connecting to a known contract.  \n- Slight variation in scores (0.1 to 0.2) reflects conservative assumptions, but no actual vulnerabilities are present.\n\n---\n\n### Logical and Reasoning Checks:\n- All reports agree that this is boilerplate code with no malicious intent.  \n- No report claims or hints at potential vulnerabilities based on assumptions; all emphasize the standard nature of the code.\n\n---\n\n### Consistency and Reasonableness:\n- The scores are consistent with the analysis; all indicate minimal or no security concern.\n\n---\n\n### Final Assessment:\n- **Malware Score:** **0** (no malicious behavior detected)  \n- **Obfuscated Score:** **0** (clear, straightforward code)  \n- **Risk Score:** **0.1 to 0.2** (very low, as the code is a standard contract factory)\n\n---\n\n### My Overall Estimation:\nConsidering all reports and the code content, I would assign:\n- **Malware:** **0**  \n- **Obfuscated:** **0**  \n- **Risk:** **0.1** (reflecting the inherent risks in deploying any smart contract, but nothing specific here)\n\n**Conclusion:**  \nThis code is a typical, auto-generated Ethereum contract factory with no signs of malicious activity or security issues.
